claim: No existing platform combines all target features: anonymous, text-only, no replies, single searchable aggregated feed, no login.
AnonymousPosting.site is the closest operational analog but remains extremely small-scale with no search functionality.
Telegra.ph received 19.03M monthly visits as of May 2026, up 23.62% month-over-month, primarily from Russia (32%).
Yik Yak raised $73 million and was valued at ~$400 million at its 2014 peak, with 1.8M downloads/month.
Yik Yak's downloads dropped 76% YoY by late 2016, and it sold to Square for $1 million in 2017.
Yik Yak's mandatory handle requirement in 2016 triggered user revolt and was reverted, but caused irreparable damage.
Yik Yak was revived in August 2021 by anonymous ownership group and later acquired by Sidechat in March 2023.
Secret raised $35M from Kleiner Perkins and Google Ventures, peaked at 15M users, then shut down in 18 months.
Secret's friend-based anonymity model created a paradox of enough anonymity for cruelty but enough identifiability for paranoia.
Secret employed 90 full-time moderators but still could not contain abuse.
Whisper raised $60M, was valued at $200M, had 10M MAU in 2015, and quietly went offline in early 2025.
Whisper had significant CSAM problems — undercover agents posing as minors were contacted by offenders in multiple federal stings.
4chan has 22M+ monthly visitors, ~20 volunteer moderators, and minimal content moderation focused on illegal content only.
PostSecret's physical postcard format created friction filtering trolls; the 2011 mobile app became toxic within 3 months.
NGL reached #1 on US App Store in June 2022, sent fake computer-generated messages, and was fined $5M by FTC.
NGL's AI moderation claims were false — testing showed phrases like 'You're fat' bypassed filters.
The ALPACA scheme achieves asymptotically constant online cost and proof size for anonymous blocklisting via zero-knowledge proofs.
Rate-Limiting Nullifier (RLN) enables zk-proof rate limiting without revealing identity, deployed in Waku messaging protocol.
Section 230 provides US platforms broad immunity for third-party content but does not apply to federal criminal law, copyright, or FOSTA-SESTA.
DSA applies tiered obligations based on platform size; micro enterprises face lighter requirements but must implement notice-and-action mechanisms.
Ofcom has authority to fine UK-accessible platforms up to £18M or 10% of global revenue under the Online Safety Act.
Telegram founder Pavel Durov was arrested in France in 2024 over platform's failure to cooperate with law enforcement on illegal content.
IP rate limiting combined with browser fingerprinting and behavioral signals stops the majority of automated abuse.
Proof-of-work stamps (Hashcash-style) throttle mass posting without requiring identity — formalized in Nostr NIP-13.
Without replies, upvotes, or interaction mechanisms, there's no feedback loop driving user retention on anonymous platforms.
Anonymous platforms have historically failed to monetize effectively due to lack of user profiles and identity data for advertising.
Removing replies could theoretically prevent toxic dynamics that killed Yik Yak by creating a 'write-only' environment.
